content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 22, 1991

Ann Miller Ravel
Chief Assistant County Counsel
County of Santa Clara
County Government Center, East Wing
70 West Hedding Street
San Jose, CA 95110-1770

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-91-354

Dear Ms. Ravel:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you on behalf of Santa Clara County Supervisor Ron Gonzales
concerning the supervisor's obligations under the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹
This letter confirms that your letter dated July 19, 1991
accurately summarizes the telephone advice I provided to you on
that date.²

As I stated in our telephone conversation of July 19, 1991,
Section 87100 of the Act prohibits any public official at any
level of state or local government from making, participating in
making or in any way attempting to use his official position to
influence a governmental decision in which the official knows or
has reason the official has a financial interest. Section
87103(b) specifies that an official has a financial interest in a
decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
have a material financial effect any real property in which the

_______________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 This letter should not be regarded as a confirmation of all
aspects of County Counsel Steven Woodside's attached memorandum.
In a conversation with Chief Deputy County Counsel Robert J.
Menifee, it was determined that issues concerning other
potentially related decisions could be discussed separately if
such a discussion was necessary.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-91-354
Page 2




public official has a direct or indirect interest worth $1,000 or
more.

     You stated that the decision in question concerned the
possible extension of a light rail line on a route within 300 to
2,500 feet of the supervisor's property. As stated in our
telephone conversation, since the supervisor's real property is
located outside a radius of 300 feet from the project site and
within a radius of 2,500 feet, the supervisor may participate
provided the decision will not affect the fair market value of the
supervisor's property by $10,000 or more, or the rental value of
the property by $1,000 or more per 12 month period. (Regulation
18702.3(a)(3).)

     After my initial conversation with Mr. Woodside, the County
obtained an appraisal of the supervisor's property which took into
account the factors required by the Act (Regulation 18702.3(d)) as
well as information obtained from real estate brokers.  This
appraisal concluded that the fair market value of the property in
question would not be affected either positively or negatively by
the amount set forth in Regulation 18702.3(a)(3).  Based on these
facts I advised that the supervisor may participate in the
decision in question.3

     I trust that this answers your questions.  If you have any
further questions regarding this matter, please feel free to
contact this office at (916) 322-5901.4

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        By:  John W. Wallace
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JWW:aa




3     Please note that the Commission does not act as the finder of
fact in providing advice.  (In re Ogelsby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)
This letter is based on the facts presented to us.

4     Copies of Commission regulations, opinions and advice letters
may be obtained from the Commission at a cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a Santa Clara County supervisor with real property located 300-2,500 feet from a proposed light rail project site may participate in the governmental decision regarding the project extension under the Political Reform Act's conflict-of-interest provisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The supervisor may participate in the decision provided the project will not affect the fair market value of the property by $10,000 or more or rental value by $1,000 or more per 12-month period. Based on an appraisal concluding no material financial effect at the threshold amounts, the supervisor may participate.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers but contains clear legal question and FPPC's substantive advice embedded in letter body. This is a confirmation letter of prior telephone advice, dated July 22, 1991. File No. A-91-354. Addressed to Ann Ravel, Chief Assistant County Counsel, Santa Clara County.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a Santa Clara County supervisor with real property located 300-2,500 feet from a proposed light rail project site may participate in the governmental decision regarding the project extension under the Political Reform Act's conflict-of-interest provisions.

CONCLUSION: The supervisor may participate in the decision provided the project will not affect the fair market value of the property by $10,000 or more or rental value by $1,000 or more per 12-month period. Based on an appraisal concluding no material financial effect at the threshold amounts, the supervisor may participate.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice to Santa Clara County regarding Supervisor Ron Gonzales' ability to participate in a light rail project decision affecting property within 300-2,500 feet of his real estate, determining participation is permitted based on appraisal evidence of no material financial impact exceeding statutory thresholds.
